summary: This brief post marks the 20th anniversary of Windows 1.0 in 2005, sharing screenshots of the original release. Atwood notes his personal history with Windows, having started with Windows 3.0 after using an Amiga. He admits being initially unimpressed with Windows, only becoming a believer with the release of Windows 3.1 and Windows For Workgroups 3.11. The post serves as a nostalgic reflection on how far Windows had come in two decades.
key_points: Atwood's first Windows experience was version 3.0, which failed to impress him compared to his Amiga background
Windows 3.1 and Windows For Workgroups 3.11 were the versions that finally won him over
money_quotes: Coming from an Amiga background, I was unimpressed.
It wasn't until Windows 3.1 and Windows For Workgroups 3.11 that I actually started to believe Microsoft had something worth using.
key_insight: Windows took several iterations before it became a platform worth taking seriously, even for those who would later become its advocates.
